basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Al-ʿAnkabūt — “The Spider” — makes trial the touchstone of faith: “Do people think they will be left to say ‘We believe’ without being tested? We have already tested those before them.” For Qushayrī, each one's worth is measured by his trial: whoever grows in true meaning sees his trial grow, and the fitna is the very proof of love. The sūra unfolds the kindness owed to parents — yet no obedience in idolatry — then the long gallery of tested prophets: Noah a thousand years save fifty among his people, Abraham calling to worship and cast into the fire from which God saves him, Lot and the emigration to the Lord, Shuʿayb at Madyan, ʿĀd and Thamūd, Korah, Pharaoh, and Hāmān — each seized for his own sin. At the heart of the sūra shines the famous parable: those who take protectors apart from God are like the spider that takes a house, and the frailest of houses is the spider's house — every support taken apart from God is but a web without foundation, walls, or roof. It exalts the recitation of the Book and the prayer that restrains from indecency, the remembrance of God “greater” than all, the vast earth offered for emigration, the provision assured to every creature, patience and tawakkul. It is sealed on the promise to the strivers: “Those who strive for Us, We shall guide them to Our paths” — for, says Qushayrī, those who adorned their outward by striving had their inward beautified by contemplations.
themes: trial as the proof of faith
prayer and the remembrance of God

versets.ar: الم
versets.tr: alif lām mīm
versets.trad: Alif. Lām. Mīm.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reads these three letters as a cipher of the divine mystery. The alif points to the Unique in His absolute independence: all things attach to Him as all letters attach to the alif, while it attaches to nothing. The lām, the letter closest to the alif by its upright stature, yet joins to others: the lām-alif alone forms a single letter made of two — an image of the meeting of servant and Real. As for the mīm, it evokes the particle “min” (from): from the Lord come creation and grace, from the servant the service of the Real.

mots.def: isolated letters opening the sūra, cipher of the mystery per Qushayrī.

versets.ar: أَحَسِبَ النَّاسُ أَن يُتْرَكُوا أَن يَقُولُوا آمَنَّا وَهُمْ لَا يُفْتَنُونَ
versets.tr: a-ḥasiba n-nāsu an yutrakū an yaqūlū āmannā wa-hum lā yuftanūna
versets.trad: Do people think they will be left to say “We believe!” without being tested?
versets.commentaire: The mere claim to faith does not suffice without the trial that verifies it. Qushayrī: each one's worth is measured by his trial; whoever grows in true meaning sees his trial grow. He distributes the trials by degree: for souls, the wrenching away from sloth toward the finest of works; for hearts, the quest and the true thought toward attesting the Oneness; for spirits, the stripping away of all love but His; for the innermost secrets, retreat into the contemplation of unveiling, by patience under the effects of the theophany until one is annihilated in it. And the harshest trial, he says, is to keep the Oneness pure when the witness of the Real overwhelms.

mots.def: interrogation of rebuke: do they reckon, presume?

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ فَتَنَّا الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۖ فَلَيَعْلَمَنَّ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ صَدَقُوا وَلَيَعْلَمَنَّ الْكَاذِبِينَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad fatannā lladhīna min qablihim fa-la-yaʿlamanna llāhu lladhīna ṣadaqū wa-la-yaʿlamanna l-kādhibīna
versets.trad: We have already tested those before them; God surely knows those who are truthful, and He surely knows the liars.
versets.commentaire: God spared none the trial, so that patience in adversity might appear, or its opposite, vexation; and gratitude in ease, or its opposite, ingratitude. Qushayrī distinguishes three kinds: he who is patient in trial and grateful in favor — that is the mark of the truthful; he who complains, neither patient nor grateful — that is the liar; and he who, even in ease, prefers not to enjoy the gift and yearns for trial, finding the taste of suffering sweet — that one is the highest of all.

mots.def: to try, put to the crucible.
to be true in word and life.

versets.ar: أَمْ حَسِبَ الَّذِينَ يَعْمَلُونَ السَّيِّئَاتِ أَن يَسْبِقُونَا ۚ سَاءَ مَا يَحْكُمُونَ
versets.tr: am ḥasiba lladhīna yaʿmalūna s-sayyiʾāti an yasbiqūnā sāʾa mā yaḥkumūna
versets.trad: Or do those who commit evil deeds suppose they can outstrip Us? Evil is the judgment they make.
versets.commentaire: They transgress, then decree their own salvation: what an evil judgment! How shall he escape the punishment who has cast off the cloak of piety? Qushayrī: they imagine there is no gathering, no resurrection, no reckoning; they let themselves be deceived by Our respite today, supposing themselves safe and forgotten by Us.

mots.racine: ع م ل / س و ء

mots.def: to get ahead, escape the grasp.

mots.racine: س و ء / ح ك م

versets.ar: مَن كَانَ يَرْجُو لِقَاءَ اللَّهِ فَإِنَّ أَجَلَ اللَّهِ لَآتٍ ۚ وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
versets.tr: man kāna yarjū liqāʾa llāhi fa-inna ajala llāhi la-ātin wa-huwa s-samīʿu l-ʿalīmu
versets.trad: Whoever hopes for the meeting with God — God's appointed term is surely coming; and He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds hope in degrees: he who today fears the punishment will find tomorrow the promised security; he who hopes for reward will see its fruit; and he who spends his life in hope of Our meeting, We shall grant him to behold Us and deliver him from absence and separation. He glosses the Names: “the All-Hearing” of the moaning of those who long for Him; “the All-Knowing” of the yearning of the distraught lovers.

mots.def: to await with hope, to desire.
the vision, the encounter in the hereafter.

mots.racine: س م ع / ع ل م

versets.ar: وَمَن جَاهَدَ فَإِنَّمَا يُجَاهِدُ لِنَفْسِهِ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَغَنِيٌّ عَنِ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-man jāhada fa-innamā yujāhidu li-nafsihi inna llāha la-ghaniyyun ʿani l-ʿālamīna
versets.trad: Whoever strives, strives only for himself. Truly God is free of need of all the worlds.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he who does good in truth seeks the salvation of his own soul and reaps its felicity; he who does evil draws punishment upon himself and earns his own wretchedness. And on the divine self-sufficiency he adds: the reward of the obedient returns to them, the punishment of the rebellious falls back upon them; as for the Real, in His glory, no accord adorns Him and no rupture mars Him.

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ لَنُكَفِّرَنَّ عَنْهُمْ سَيِّئَاتِهِمْ وَلَنَجْزِيَنَّهُمْ أَحْسَنَ الَّذِي كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti la-nukaffiranna ʿanhum sayyiʾātihim wa-la-najziyannahum aḥsana lladhī kānū yaʿmalūna
versets.trad: And those who believe and do righteous deeds — We shall surely efface their evil deeds and shall reward them for the best of what they did.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever lifts toward Us a single step gains from Us a step; whoever forsakes a desire for Us finds from Us a chosen purity. Thus is their share of good things made full and their fault in their slips forgiven. Such, he says, is the way God has established, the working of His decree and His judgment.

versets.ar: وَوَصَّيْنَا الْإِنسَانَ بِوَالِدَيْهِ حُسْنًا ۖ وَإِن جَاهَدَاكَ لِتُشْرِكَ بِي مَا لَيْسَ لَكَ بِهِ عِلْمٌ فَلَا تُطِعْهُمَا ۚ إِلَيَّ مَرْجِعُكُمْ فَأُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-waṣṣaynā l-insāna bi-wālidayhi ḥusnan wa-in jāhadāka li-tushrika bī mā laysa laka bihi ʿilmun fa-lā tuṭiʿhumā ilayya marjiʿukum fa-unabbiʾukum bimā kuntum taʿmalūna
versets.trad: We have enjoined upon man kindness toward his parents; but if they strive to make you associate with Me that of which you have no knowledge, do not obey them. To Me is your return, and I shall inform you of what you used to do.
versets.commentaire: God commands honoring the right of parents to recall the greatness of the right of nurturing. Qushayrī reasons: if the nurture of parents, when good, merits such regard, what then of the right of God, whose benefaction envelops the servant and whose ancient grace distinguished him before and after? But if they urge association, beware of obeying them; repel them with gentleness, oppose them with kindness.

mots.def: shirk: to associate another with God.

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ لَنُدْخِلَنَّهُمْ فِي الصَّالِحِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti la-nudkhilannahum fī ṣ-ṣāliḥīna
versets.trad: And those who believe and do righteous deeds — We shall surely admit them among the righteous.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: We shall join them to those who made themselves righteous before them, for it is Our known way to unite the like with its like and to bring the same under the rule of its same. The soul that has purified itself joins the company of the purified.

mots.def: to bring in, cause to enter.
the upright, those who amended themselves.

versets.ar: وَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَن يَقُولُ آمَنَّا بِاللَّهِ فَإِذَا أُوذِيَ فِي اللَّهِ جَعَلَ فِتْنَةَ النَّاسِ كَعَذَابِ اللَّهِ وَلَئِن جَاءَ نَصْرٌ مِّن رَّبِّكَ لَيَقُولُنَّ إِنَّا كُنَّا مَعَكُمْ ۚ أَوَلَيْسَ اللَّهُ بِأَعْلَمَ بِمَا فِي صُدُورِ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-mina n-nāsi man yaqūlu āmannā bi-llāhi fa-idhā ūdhiya fī llāhi jaʿala fitnata n-nāsi ka-ʿadhābi llāhi wa-la-in jāʾa naṣrun min rabbika la-yaqūlunna innā kunnā maʿakum a-wa-laysa llāhu bi-aʿlama bimā fī ṣudūri l-ʿālamīna
versets.trad: Among the people are those who say, “We believe in God,” but when one of them is harmed for God's sake, he treats the persecution of men as if it were the punishment of God. Yet if help comes from your Lord, they say, “Truly we were with you!” Does not God know best what is in the breasts of all creatures?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: trials bring out the substance of men; each one's worth is revealed in the hour of his trial. He distinguishes: he who grieves over the loss of this world, the death of a kinsman or a beloved, is of small measure, and many are his like; but he who suffers in God and for God is precious, and rare are his peers — few in number, but lofty in rank. The true believer, he says, absorbs the offense without spilling into complaint, like the earth on which every filth is cast, yet brings forth only greenery and delight.

mots.racine: ف ت ن / ن و س
ص د ر / ع ل م

versets.ar: وَلَيَعْلَمَنَّ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَلَيَعْلَمَنَّ الْمُنَافِقِينَ
versets.tr: wa-la-yaʿlamanna llāhu lladhīna āmanū wa-la-yaʿlamanna l-munāfiqīna
versets.trad: And God will surely make known those who believe, and He will surely make known the hypocrites.
versets.commentaire: Trial separates the sincere from the pretender. Qushayrī cites the verse: “When tears mingle upon the cheeks, then is distinguished who truly weeps from who only feigns to weep.” The crucible of affliction reveals who believes in truth and who bears only its appearance.

mots.def: to know manifestly, bring to light.
those who show faith and hide denial.

versets.ar: وَقَالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّبِعُوا سَبِيلَنَا وَلْنَحْمِلْ خَطَايَاكُمْ وَمَا هُم بِحَامِلِينَ مِنْ خَطَايَاهُم مِّن شَيْءٍ ۖ إِنَّهُمْ لَكَاذِبُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla lladhīna kafarū li-lladhīna āmanū ttabiʿū sabīlanā wa-l-naḥmil khaṭāyākum wa-mā hum bi-ḥāmilīna min khaṭāyāhum min shayʾin innahum la-kādhibūna
versets.trad: Those who disbelieve say to those who believe: “Follow our way, and we will bear your sins” — yet they will bear nothing of their sins. Truly they are liars!
versets.commentaire: They guarantee what they will not fulfill and break their promise. Qushayrī: not only will they remove nothing of others' sins, but they will add to the burden of their own soul the load of the evil they enjoined — for he who calls to evil bears his own burden and that of whoever imitates him. Their promises to the believers are worth no more than the deceitful promises of faithless men.

mots.racine: ت ب ع / س ب ل

versets.ar: وَلَيَحْمِلُنَّ أَثْقَالَهُمْ وَأَثْقَالًا مَّعَ أَثْقَالِهِمْ ۖ وَلَيُسْأَلُنَّ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ عَمَّا كَانُوا يَفْتَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-la-yaḥmilunna athqālahum wa-athqālan maʿa athqālihim wa-la-yusʾalunna yawma l-qiyāmati ʿammā kānū yaftarūna
versets.trad: They will surely bear their own burdens, and other burdens along with their own. And on the Day of Resurrection they will be questioned about what they used to fabricate.
versets.commentaire: To these, says Qushayrī, will be joined the people of vain claims, those who feign the likeness of the men of realities. He cites: “Whoever adorns himself with what he is not, the test exposes his pretension.” And the verse echoes: “Bring your proof, if you are truthful” — but alas, how far they are from it!

mots.def: the day of rising and judgment.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا نُوحًا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِ فَلَبِثَ فِيهِمْ أَلْفَ سَنَةٍ إِلَّا خَمْسِينَ عَامًا فَأَخَذَهُمُ الطُّوفَانُ وَهُمْ ظَالِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad arsalnā nūḥan ilā qawmihi fa-labitha fīhim alfa sanatin illā khamsīna ʿāman fa-akhadhahumu ṭ-ṭūfānu wa-hum ẓālimūna
versets.trad: We sent Noah to his people, and he remained among them a thousand years save fifty; then the flood seized them while they were wrongdoers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the very length of his stay among them increased only their doubt about his cause, their ignorance of his state, and their suspicion of his sincerity; while Noah, for his part, increased only in counsel and in patience for God. God informed him that none would believe save the few already won; commanded him to build the ark; and drowned the deniers, sparing not one: no change touches His way in the support of His religion.

versets.ar: فَأَنجَيْنَاهُ وَأَصْحَابَ السَّفِينَةِ وَجَعَلْنَاهَا آيَةً لِّلْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: fa-anjaynāhu wa-aṣḥāba s-safīnati wa-jaʿalnāhā āyatan li-l-ʿālamīna
versets.trad: But We saved him and the companions of the ark, and made it a sign for all the worlds.
versets.commentaire: The saving of Noah and the ark becomes a perpetual sign. Qushayrī sees in it the verification of the divine promise: He was truthful in His promise and gave victory to His servant, so that the deliverance of the believers remains a lesson offered to every generation that knows how to ponder.

mots.racine: ص ح ب / س ف ن

versets.ar: وَإِبْرَاهِيمَ إِذْ قَالَ لِقَوْمِهِ اعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ وَاتَّقُوهُ ۖ ذَٰلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ibrāhīma idh qāla li-qawmihi ʿbudū llāha wa-ttaqūhu dhālikum khayrun lakum in kuntum taʿlamūna
versets.trad: And Abraham, when he said to his people: “Worship God and fear Him! That is better for you, if only you knew!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī recalls how Abraham set the proof before his people and guided them to the straight way, but they persisted in their denial and grew fanatical for the idols they worshiped. They plotted a scheme against him, but God turned that plot back upon them as a stratagem and a deceptive respite; his counsel did not penetrate them, his exhortation found in them no passage.

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا تَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ أَوْثَانًا وَتَخْلُقُونَ إِفْكًا ۚ إِنَّ الَّذِينَ تَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ لَا يَمْلِكُونَ لَكُمْ رِزْقًا فَابْتَغُوا عِندَ اللَّهِ الرِّزْقَ وَاعْبُدُوهُ وَاشْكُرُوا لَهُ ۖ إِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ
versets.tr: innamā taʿbudūna min dūni llāhi awthānan wa-takhluqūna ifkan inna lladhīna taʿbudūna min dūni llāhi lā yamlikūna lakum rizqan fa-btaghū ʿinda llāhi r-rizqa wa-ʿbudūhu wa-shkurū lahu ilayhi turjaʿūna
versets.trad: You worship, apart from God, only idols, and you fabricate a falsehood. Those you worship apart from God hold no provision for you. So seek provision from God, worship Him, and give Him thanks. To Him you shall be returned.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: one knows not which is uglier — your deeds in worshiping these lifeless things, or your lying words about them — they who hold for you neither benefit nor protection. On “seek provision from God,” he teaches: seeking sustenance comes through perseverance in prayer, for prayer opens the door of provision; and through awareness of one's destitution, for then desire turns toward God to draw it. Gratitude is due, he concludes, because He has freed you from the care of provision so that you might be free for His worship.

mots.racine: خ ل ق / ء ف ك

versets.ar: وَإِن تُكَذِّبُوا فَقَدْ كَذَّبَ أُمَمٌ مِّن قَبْلِكُمْ ۖ وَمَا عَلَى الرَّسُولِ إِلَّا الْبَلَاغُ الْمُبِينُ
versets.tr: wa-in tukadhdhibū fa-qad kadhdhaba umamun min qablikum wa-mā ʿalā r-rasūli illā l-balāghu l-mubīnu
versets.trad: If you cry lies, communities before you cried lies too. The Messenger's duty is only the clear proclamation.
versets.commentaire: The consequence of denial falls back upon the denier. Qushayrī: upon the Messenger, once his transmission is accomplished without any negligence, in a manifest way, lies nothing more — otherwise he would step outside his charge of exhortation. And in the fate of the deniers of old lies a lesson for those who come after them.

mots.racine: ب ل غ / ب ي ن
mots.def: the explicit proclamation of the message.

versets.ar: أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا كَيْفَ يُبْدِئُ اللَّهُ الْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُ ۚ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى اللَّهِ يَسِيرٌ
versets.tr: a-wa-lam yaraw kayfa yubdiʾu llāhu l-khalqa thumma yuʿīduhu inna dhālika ʿalā llāhi yasīrun
versets.trad: Do they not see how God originates creation, then renews it? Truly that is easy for God.
versets.commentaire: Their doubt concerned resurrection; God argues by the cyclic return of the seasons. Qushayrī widens it: just as the seasons bring back the common states, so the states of the believers renew themselves — the grip of the soul's desires, then their dissipation; assiduity in obedience, then languor; then the return; then the awakening through repentance. And the masters of hearts, he says, see their states succeed one another: contraction and expansion, awe and intimacy, unveiling and veiling, subsistence and annihilation, intoxication and sobriety.

versets.ar: قُلْ سِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ فَانظُرُوا كَيْفَ بَدَأَ الْخَلْقَ ۚ ثُمَّ اللَّهُ يُنشِئُ النَّشْأَةَ الْآخِرَةَ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
versets.tr: qul sīrū fī l-arḍi fa-nẓurū kayfa badaʾa l-khalqa thumma llāhu yunshiʾu n-nashʾata l-ākhirata inna llāha ʿalā kulli shayʾin qadīrun
versets.trad: Say: “Travel through the earth and behold how He originated creation. Then God will bring forth the final creation. Truly God has power over all things!”
versets.commentaire: The call to travel the earth is a summons to ponder the signs. Qushayrī cites, on the return of states: “Every river in which water has flowed — to it the water shall one day return.” As the first creation attests the second, so the journey of the gaze attests the power that will bring forth the final creation.

mots.racine: ن ش ء / ء خ ر

versets.ar: يُعَذِّبُ مَن يَشَاءُ وَيَرْحَمُ مَن يَشَاءُ ۖ وَإِلَيْهِ تُقْلَبُونَ
versets.tr: yuʿadhdhibu man yashāʾu wa-yarḥamu man yashāʾu wa-ilayhi tuqlabūna
versets.trad: He punishes whom He wills and shows mercy to whom He wills, and to Him you will be turned back.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the countless variety of punishments and graces: He punishes whom He wills by abandonment, and shows mercy to whom He wills by faith; He punishes by craving and shows mercy by contentment; He punishes by the scattering of concern and shows mercy by the gathering of resolve; He punishes by plunging into the darkness of self-contrivance, and shows mercy by making one contemplate the flow of the decree; He punishes by consigning a man to himself, and shows mercy by Himself taking up his fair guardianship.

versets.ar: وَمَا أَنتُم بِمُعْجِزِينَ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِي السَّمَاءِ ۖ وَمَا لَكُم مِّن دُونِ اللَّهِ مِن وَلِيٍّ وَلَا نَصِيرٍ
versets.tr: wa-mā antum bi-muʿjizīna fī l-arḍi wa-lā fī s-samāʾi wa-mā lakum min dūni llāhi min waliyyin wa-lā naṣīrin
versets.trad: You cannot frustrate Him on the earth or in the heaven, and you have, apart from God, neither protector nor helper.
versets.commentaire: None escapes the divine grasp. Qushayrī: We turn the whole in the hollow of the hand and run upon them the rulings of the decree — whether they deny or attest the Oneness, whether they turn toward Him or away. Apart from God, there is neither protector nor helper.

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ وَلِقَائِهِ أُولَٰئِكَ يَئِسُوا مِن رَّحْمَتِي وَأُولَٰئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna kafarū bi-āyāti llāhi wa-liqāʾihi ulāʾika yaʾisū min raḥmatī wa-ulāʾika lahum ʿadhābun alīmun
versets.trad: Those who disbelieve in God's signs and in the meeting with Him — they despair of My mercy, and for them is a painful punishment.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī condenses: their punishment was hastened by the very fact that they despaired of His mercy — and no punishment is harsher than that. Despair of grace is, in itself, already the torment.

mots.racine: ك ف ر / ء ي ي
mots.def: to reject the verses and proofs.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ء ل م

versets.ar: فَمَا كَانَ جَوَابَ قَوْمِهِ إِلَّا أَن قَالُوا اقْتُلُوهُ أَوْ حَرِّقُوهُ فَأَنجَاهُ اللَّهُ مِنَ النَّارِ ۚ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَآيَاتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: fa-mā kāna jawāba qawmihi illā an qālū qtulūhu aw ḥarriqūhu fa-anjāhu llāhu mina n-nāri inna fī dhālika la-āyātin li-qawmin yuʾminūna
versets.trad: The only answer of his people was to say: “Kill him or burn him!” But God saved him from the fire. Truly in that are signs for a people who believe!
versets.commentaire: Powerless to answer his argument, they resorted to threat and insult. Qushayrī: God turned their plot away from him, sufficed him against their stratagem, and made his proof triumph over them; He showed all their impotence and announced what would befall them — the curse, the banishment, and every form of humiliation. The fire turned to coolness is a sign for whoever believes.

mots.racine: ج و ب / ق و م

versets.ar: وَقَالَ إِنَّمَا اتَّخَذْتُم مِّن دُونِ اللَّهِ أَوْثَانًا مَّوَدَّةَ بَيْنِكُمْ فِي الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا ۖ ثُمَّ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ يَكْفُرُ بَعْضُكُم بِبَعْضٍ وَيَلْعَنُ بَعْضُكُم بَعْضًا وَمَأْوَاكُمُ النَّارُ وَمَا لَكُم مِّن نَّاصِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla innamā ttakhadhtum min dūni llāhi awthānan mawaddata baynikum fī l-ḥayāti d-dunyā thumma yawma l-qiyāmati yakfuru baʿḍukum bi-baʿḍin wa-yalʿanu baʿḍukum baʿḍan wa-maʾwākumu n-nāru wa-mā lakum min nāṣirīna
versets.trad: And he said: “You have taken, apart from God, only idols, out of mutual affection in the life of this world. Then on the Day of Resurrection you will deny one another and curse one another, and your refuge will be the Fire, and you will have no helpers.”
versets.commentaire: Abraham unveils the hidden spring of their idolatry: a mere affective complicity, a worldly bond knotted among them. This affection built on something other than God reverses, on the Day of rising, into mutual denial and cursing, Qushayrī suggests: what gathered them in illusion divides them in truth, and the common refuge becomes nothing but the Fire, with no helper.

mots.racine: ك ف ر / ب ع ض
ء و ي / ن و ر

versets.ar: فَآمَنَ لَهُ لُوطٌ ۘ وَقَالَ إِنِّي مُهَاجِرٌ إِلَىٰ رَبِّي ۖ إِنَّهُ هُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ
versets.tr: fa-āmana lahu lūṭun wa-qāla innī muhājirun ilā rabbī innahu huwa l-ʿazīzu l-ḥakīmu
versets.trad: And Lot believed in him and said: “I shall emigrate to my Lord; truly He is the Mighty, the Wise!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes Lot's emigration the model of inner exile: there is no true emigration to God save by the perfect detachment of the heart from all other than Him. The emigration of the body is a small thing beside the emigration of the heart — that of the elect — which is the going-forth from the homelands of dispersion to the open fields of gathering. To remain in separation and to dwell in the contemplation of union are, he says, irreconcilable.

mots.def: one who makes the hijra, the exile.

mots.racine: ع ز ز / ح ك م

versets.ar: وَوَهَبْنَا لَهُ إِسْحَاقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ وَجَعَلْنَا فِي ذُرِّيَّتِهِ النُّبُوَّةَ وَالْكِتَابَ وَآتَيْنَاهُ أَجْرَهُ فِي الدُّنْيَا ۖ وَإِنَّهُ فِي الْآخِرَةِ لَمِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ
versets.tr: wa-wahabnā lahu isḥāqa wa-yaʿqūba wa-jaʿalnā fī dhurriyyatihi n-nubuwwata wa-l-kitāba wa-ātaynāhu ajrahu fī d-dunyā wa-innahu fī l-ākhirati la-mina ṣ-ṣāliḥīna
versets.trad: And We bestowed upon him Isaac and Jacob, and We placed in his descendants prophethood and the Book; and We gave him his reward in this world, and in the hereafter he is among the righteous.
versets.commentaire: Because he offered counsel withholding none of his tenderness, God fulfilled his vow in his line. Qushayrī: He blessed his descendants, deposited in them the Book and prophethood, and chose them for good things, so that their works were worthy of acceptance, their states worthy of orientation toward Him, their souls devoted to His worship, their secrets to His contemplation, and their hearts to His knowledge. “Among the righteous” means, for him, nearness, drawing close, and election into intimacy.

mots.def: to grant as a free gift.

mots.racine: ن ب ء / ك ت ب

versets.ar: وَلُوطًا إِذْ قَالَ لِقَوْمِهِ إِنَّكُمْ لَتَأْتُونَ الْفَاحِشَةَ مَا سَبَقَكُم بِهَا مِنْ أَحَدٍ مِّنَ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lūṭan idh qāla li-qawmihi innakum la-taʾtūna l-fāḥishata mā sabaqakum bihā min aḥadin mina l-ʿālamīna
versets.trad: And Lot, when he said to his people: “You commit an abomination that no one in all the worlds has committed before you.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī censures their ignominious conduct and their audacity toward God: what they neglected of the seemly and what they committed of the blameworthy — leaving the debauched free in their debauchery, not restraining their hand, with no shame that people should see their vileness. He adds their scant regard for elders and great ones, their postponing of repentance, and their boasting of the fault.

mots.racine: ء ت ي / ف ح ش

versets.ar: أَئِنَّكُمْ لَتَأْتُونَ الرِّجَالَ وَتَقْطَعُونَ السَّبِيلَ وَتَأْتُونَ فِي نَادِيكُمُ الْمُنكَرَ ۖ فَمَا كَانَ جَوَابَ قَوْمِهِ إِلَّا أَن قَالُوا ائْتِنَا بِعَذَابِ اللَّهِ إِن كُنتَ مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ
versets.tr: a-innakum la-taʾtūna r-rijāla wa-taqṭaʿūna s-sabīla wa-taʾtūna fī nādīkumu l-munkara fa-mā kāna jawāba qawmihi illā an qālū ʾtinā bi-ʿadhābi llāhi in kunta mina ṣ-ṣādiqīna
versets.trad: Do you approach men, cut off the highway, and commit reprehensible acts in your assemblies?” The only answer of his people was to say: “Bring upon us the punishment of God, if you are among the truthful!”
versets.commentaire: Still according to Qushayrī, their sole reply was to demand the hastening of the punishment — insolent bravado. Then there fell upon them what destroyed them, and those who had shared in their corruption. The challenge thrown at the prophet seals their ruin.

mots.racine: ء ت ي / ر ج ل
ق ط ع / س ب ل
ن د و / ن ك ر
ء ت ي / ع ذ ب

versets.ar: قَالَ رَبِّ انصُرْنِي عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْمُفْسِدِينَ
versets.tr: qāla rabbi nṣurnī ʿalā l-qawmi l-mufsidīna
versets.trad: He said: “My Lord, help me against the corrupting people!”
versets.commentaire: Before the obstinacy of the corrupting people, Lot turns to none but God. The prophet's invocation, when exhortation has exhausted every passage, is the avowal that help belongs to the Real alone: he commits his cause to the One who upholds the weak and breaks the oppressors.

versets.ar: وَلَمَّا جَاءَتْ رُسُلُنَا إِبْرَاهِيمَ بِالْبُشْرَىٰ قَالُوا إِنَّا مُهْلِكُو أَهْلِ هَٰذِهِ الْقَرْيَةِ ۖ إِنَّ أَهْلَهَا كَانُوا ظَالِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lammā jāʾat rusulunā ibrāhīma bi-l-bushrā qālū innā muhlikū ahli hādhihi l-qaryati inna ahlahā kānū ẓālimīna
versets.trad: And when Our messengers brought Abraham the good tidings, they said: “We are about to destroy the people of this town; truly its people are wrongdoers,”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī recounts that Abraham mistook them and took them for guests, offering the roasted calf according to his custom of hospitality. When they disclosed their design to destroy Lot's people, he interceded for Lot — proof that his word on behalf of that town hung upon the presence of the righteous one within it.

mots.racine: ه ل ك / ء ه ل

versets.ar: قَالَ إِنَّ فِيهَا لُوطًا ۚ قَالُوا نَحْنُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَن فِيهَا ۖ لَنُنَجِّيَنَّهُ وَأَهْلَهُ إِلَّا امْرَأَتَهُ كَانَتْ مِنَ الْغَابِرِينَ
versets.tr: qāla inna fīhā lūṭan qālū naḥnu aʿlamu biman fīhā la-nunajjiyannahu wa-ahlahu illā mraʾatahu kānat mina l-ghābirīna
versets.trad: He said: “But Lot is in it!” They said: “We know better who is in it; we shall surely save him and his family, except his wife, who is to be among those who remain behind.”
versets.commentaire: Abraham's solicitude for Lot draws from Qushayrī a truth about the decree: had God willed to destroy Lot — though innocent — it would have been no injustice; for if it were abominable, Abraham, with all his knowledge, would not have hesitated to plead for him. But God has the right to punish whom He punishes and to pardon whom He pardons.

mots.sens: know better who is in it

versets.ar: وَلَمَّا أَن جَاءَتْ رُسُلُنَا لُوطًا سِيءَ بِهِمْ وَضَاقَ بِهِمْ ذَرْعًا وَقَالُوا لَا تَخَفْ وَلَا تَحْزَنْ ۖ إِنَّا مُنَجُّوكَ وَأَهْلَكَ إِلَّا امْرَأَتَكَ كَانَتْ مِنَ الْغَابِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lammā an jāʾat rusulunā lūṭan sīʾa bihim wa-ḍāqa bihim dharʿan wa-qālū lā takhaf wa-lā taḥzan innā munajjūka wa-ahlaka illā mraʾataka kānat mina l-ghābirīna
versets.trad: And when Our messengers came to Lot, he was distressed for them and felt powerless to protect them. They said: “Do not fear and do not grieve; we shall save you and your family, except your wife, who is to be among those who remain behind.
versets.commentaire: Lot was anguished, not knowing they were angels, fearing for them the perversity of his people: his heart contracted for the love of God. Qushayrī draws from this a law of the path: the servant is nearest to deliverance when the trial reaches its peak; then comes the lifting of the trial, for he becomes constrained, and God has promised the constrained a swift response. Thus Lot, his heart wrung that night, soon found the way out.

mots.racine: ض ي ق / ذ ر ع
خ و ف / ح ز ن

versets.ar: إِنَّا مُنزِلُونَ عَلَىٰ أَهْلِ هَٰذِهِ الْقَرْيَةِ رِجْزًا مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ بِمَا كَانُوا يَفْسُقُونَ
versets.tr: innā munzilūna ʿalā ahli hādhihi l-qaryati rijzan mina s-samāʾi bimā kānū yafsuqūna
versets.trad: We shall send down upon the people of this town a punishment from heaven, for the perversity they used to commit.”
versets.commentaire: The cataclysm falls from heaven for the price of their perversity: the sanction matches the crime exactly. The punishment that descends from above answers the corruption that rose from the earth — divine justice makes the penalty correspond to the fault, with no excess and no wrong.

versets.ar: وَلَقَد تَّرَكْنَا مِنْهَا آيَةً بَيِّنَةً لِّقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad taraknā minhā āyatan bayyinatan li-qawmin yaʿqilūna
versets.trad: And We left of it a clear sign for a people who use reason.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, briefly, says the essential: whoever wishes to take heed will find in this story something to ponder. The ruins of the punished town remain a warning offered to the intelligence that knows how to read the signs.

mots.racine: ء ي ي / ب ي ن

versets.ar: وَإِلَىٰ مَدْيَنَ أَخَاهُمْ شُعَيْبًا فَقَالَ يَا قَوْمِ اعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ وَارْجُوا الْيَوْمَ الْآخِرَ وَلَا تَعْثَوْا فِي الْأَرْضِ مُفْسِدِينَ
versets.tr: wa-ilā madyana akhāhum shuʿayban fa-qāla yā qawmi ʿbudū llāha wa-rjū l-yawma l-ākhira wa-lā taʿthaw fī l-arḍi mufsidīna
versets.trad: And to Madyan their brother Shuʿayb. He said: “O my people, worship God and place your hope in the Last Day, and do not spread corruption upon the earth.”
themes: the call and the hope of the Last Day
versets.commentaire: With Shuʿayb, Qushayrī encloses the whole series of punished peoples: they wove one upon the other's pattern, walked the same way, refused the counsel, cared nothing for opposing their messengers; then God destroyed them all, in fulfillment of His way of helping the weak and crushing the oppressors.

mots.racine: ر ج و / ء خ ر
mots.def: to place hope in the hereafter.

versets.ar: فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ الرَّجْفَةُ فَأَصْبَحُوا فِي دَارِهِمْ جَاثِمِينَ
versets.tr: fa-kadhdhabūhu fa-akhadhathumu r-rajfatu fa-aṣbaḥū fī dārihim jāthimīna
versets.trad: But they cried him a liar, and the earthquake seized them, and by morning they lay lifeless in their homes.
versets.commentaire: Denial summons the punishment at once: they cried the messenger a liar, and the tremor seized them, leaving them by morning inert in their own houses. For Qushayrī, they join the ranks of those who did not heed the counsel and were swept away by the immutable law of the prophets' support.

versets.ar: وَعَادًا وَثَمُودَ وَقَد تَّبَيَّنَ لَكُم مِّن مَّسَاكِنِهِمْ ۖ وَزَيَّنَ لَهُمُ الشَّيْطَانُ أَعْمَالَهُمْ فَصَدَّهُمْ عَنِ السَّبِيلِ وَكَانُوا مُسْتَبْصِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-ʿādan wa-thamūda wa-qad tabayyana lakum min masākinihim wa-zayyana lahumu sh-shayṭānu aʿmālahum fa-ṣaddahum ʿani s-sabīli wa-kānū mustabṣirīna
versets.trad: And ʿĀd and Thamūd — it has become clear to you from their dwellings. Satan made their deeds seem fair to them and barred them from the way, though they were endowed with sight.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī ranges ʿĀd and Thamūd in the same fabric of obstinate peoples. What the verse underscores is the trap of satanic embellishment: Satan adorned their deeds in their own eyes, turning them from the way even as they believed themselves clear-sighted — the most dangerous blindness is the one that takes itself for lucidity.

mots.racine: ز ي ن / ش ط ن

versets.ar: وَقَارُونَ وَفِرْعَوْنَ وَهَامَانَ ۖ وَلَقَدْ جَاءَهُم مُّوسَىٰ بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ فَاسْتَكْبَرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَمَا كَانُوا سَابِقِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qārūna wa-firʿawna wa-hāmāna wa-laqad jāʾahum mūsā bi-l-bayyināti fa-stakbarū fī l-arḍi wa-mā kānū sābiqīna
versets.trad: And Korah, Pharaoh, and Hāmān: Moses came to them with clear proofs, but they grew arrogant in the land, and they could not outstrip Us.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī joins Korah, Pharaoh, and Hāmān to the rest: all refused the counsel and defied their messengers, then God destroyed them all. Despite the dazzling proofs of Moses, they swelled with arrogance — but arrogance does not outstrip the decree: they could not get ahead of God's grasp.

versets.ar: فَكُلًّا أَخَذْنَا بِذَنبِهِ ۖ فَمِنْهُم مَّنْ أَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِ حَاصِبًا وَمِنْهُم مَّنْ أَخَذَتْهُ الصَّيْحَةُ وَمِنْهُم مَّنْ خَسَفْنَا بِهِ الْأَرْضَ وَمِنْهُم مَّنْ أَغْرَقْنَا ۚ وَمَا كَانَ اللَّهُ لِيَظْلِمَهُمْ وَلَٰكِن كَانُوا أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: fa-kullan akhadhnā bi-dhanbihi fa-minhum man arsalnā ʿalayhi ḥāṣiban wa-minhum man akhadhathu ṣ-ṣayḥatu wa-minhum man khasafnā bihi l-arḍa wa-minhum man aghraqnā wa-mā kāna llāhu li-yaẓlimahum wa-lākin kānū anfusahum yaẓlimūna
versets.trad: Each one We seized for his sin: upon some We sent a storm of stones; some the Cry seized; some We caused the earth to swallow; and some We drowned. God did them no wrong, but they wronged themselves.
versets.commentaire: Each people was seized for its sin by a punishment of its own. Qushayrī underscores the seal of the verse: God did them no wrong, but it was themselves they wronged. The diversity of the scourges — wind, cry, swallowing, drowning — manifests a single law: man's evil returns upon man, and divine justice is never at fault.

mots.racine: ء خ ذ / ذ ن ب

mots.def: to cause the earth to swallow.

versets.ar: مَثَلُ الَّذِينَ اتَّخَذُوا مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ أَوْلِيَاءَ كَمَثَلِ الْعَنكَبُوتِ اتَّخَذَتْ بَيْتًا ۖ وَإِنَّ أَوْهَنَ الْبُيُوتِ لَبَيْتُ الْعَنكَبُوتِ ۖ لَوْ كَانُوا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: mathalu lladhīna ttakhadhū min dūni llāhi awliyāʾa ka-mathali l-ʿankabūti ttakhadhat baytan wa-inna awhana l-buyūti la-baytu l-ʿankabūti law kānū yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: The likeness of those who take protectors apart from God is as the likeness of the spider that takes a house — and truly the frailest of houses is the spider's house, if only they knew.
themes: the frailty of every other support
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the parable: the spider weaves itself a house, but the more it spins, the more it shuts itself in and recedes from any way out: it builds, but it builds against itself — so the disbeliever toils, but it is against his own soul that he labors. And the spider's house lodges in corners, hidden away: likewise, the denier's affair is woven of concealment, while the believer acts in the open, veiling nothing. Finally it is the frailest of houses, without foundation, without walls, without roof, unable to stop the least push: such is the disbeliever, without root or base, who perceives only by illusion — but in truth, nothing.

mots.racine: ء خ ذ / و ل ي
mots.def: to take allies other than God.

mots.racine: و ه ن / ب ي ت

versets.ar: إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ مَا يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِهِ مِن شَيْءٍ ۚ وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ
versets.tr: inna llāha yaʿlamu mā yadʿūna min dūnihi min shayʾin wa-huwa l-ʿazīzu l-ḥakīmu
versets.trad: Truly God knows whatever thing they invoke apart from Him. He is the Mighty, the Wise!
versets.commentaire: God knows perfectly these objects of invocation that have no reality of help. What is invoked apart from Him is nothing — and He alone has full knowledge of it: the frail web of false protections is known to the One whose might and wisdom have no rival, the Mighty, the Wise.

mots.racine: ع ز ز / ح ك م

versets.ar: وَتِلْكَ الْأَمْثَالُ نَضْرِبُهَا لِلنَّاسِ ۖ وَمَا يَعْقِلُهَا إِلَّا الْعَالِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-tilka l-amthālu naḍribuhā li-n-nāsi wa-mā yaʿqiluhā illā l-ʿālimūna
versets.trad: These parables We set forth for mankind, but none understand them save those who know.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: all hear the parables alike, but none lends them an ear save the one whose heart flees, whose state is ungrateful, accustomed to sloth, entrenched in the homelands of failure. Understanding is not a matter of the ear but of the disposed heart.

versets.ar: خَلَقَ اللَّهُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ بِالْحَقِّ ۚ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَآيَةً لِّلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: khalaqa llāhu s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍa bi-l-ḥaqqi inna fī dhālika la-āyatan li-l-muʾminīna
versets.trad: God created the heavens and the earth in truth. Truly in that is a sign for the believers.
versets.commentaire: “In truth” means, for Qushayrī, by the true word and the true command: creation is neither play nor vanity, but bears within it the seal of the Real. And in this ordering lies a sign that only the believer knows how to receive.

mots.racine: س م و / ء ر ض

mots.def: according to the true, by design.

versets.ar: اتْلُ مَا أُوحِيَ إِلَيْكَ مِنَ الْكِتَابِ وَأَقِمِ الصَّلَاةَ ۖ إِنَّ الصَّلَاةَ تَنْهَىٰ عَنِ الْفَحْشَاءِ وَالْمُنكَرِ ۗ وَلَذِكْرُ اللَّهِ أَكْبَرُ ۗ وَاللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ مَا تَصْنَعُونَ
versets.tr: utlu mā ūḥiya ilayka mina l-kitābi wa-aqimi ṣ-ṣalāta inna ṣ-ṣalāta tanhā ʿani l-faḥshāʾi wa-l-munkari wa-la-dhikru llāhi akbaru wa-llāhu yaʿlamu mā taṣnaʿūna
versets.trad: Recite what has been revealed to you of the Book, and establish the prayer. Truly the prayer restrains from indecency and wrong, and the remembrance of God is greater. And God knows what you do.
versets.commentaire: On “prayer restrains from indecency,” Qushayrī gives several readings: the true prayer is the one that turns its performer away from the blameworthy; if a man is not turned away, then he has the form of prayer, not its reality. On remembrance, he multiplies the luminous senses: God's remembrance of you is greater than your remembrance of Him; His remembrance of you with felicity is greater than your remembrance of Him with worship; the remembrance of God is too great for any desolation to remain with it; too great for the remembrance of any creature to subsist alongside it for the rememberer.

mots.racine: ق و م / ص ل و
mots.def: to perform the rite of prayer.

mots.racine: ن ه ي / ف ح ش
mots.def: to turn away from the obscene.

mots.racine: ذ ك ر / ك ب ر
mots.sens: the remembrance of God is greater

versets.ar: وَلَا تُجَادِلُوا أَهْلَ الْكِتَابِ إِلَّا بِالَّتِي هِيَ أَحْسَنُ إِلَّا الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا مِنْهُمْ ۖ وَقُولُوا آمَنَّا بِالَّذِي أُنزِلَ إِلَيْنَا وَأُنزِلَ إِلَيْكُمْ وَإِلَٰهُنَا وَإِلَٰهُكُمْ وَاحِدٌ وَنَحْنُ لَهُ مُسْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lā tujādilū ahla l-kitābi illā bi-llatī hiya aḥsanu illā lladhīna ẓalamū minhum wa-qūlū āmannā bi-lladhī unzila ilaynā wa-unzila ilaykum wa-ilāhunā wa-ilāhukum wāḥidun wa-naḥnu lahu muslimūna
versets.trad: Do not dispute with the People of the Book except in the fairest manner — save with those of them who do wrong — and say: “We believe in what has been revealed to us and what has been revealed to you; our God and your God are One, and to Him we submit.”
themes: the oneness of the worshiped God
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws from this verse an ethic of debate: you must, toward your opponent, be clear, gentle in speech, fair in receiving the truth, firm in upholding by proof what you recognize as right, and free of any leaning carried by passion. The fair manner is no weakness, but fidelity to the truth stripped of the ego.

mots.racine: ء ل ه / و ح د
mots.sens: our God and yours is One

versets.ar: وَكَذَٰلِكَ أَنزَلْنَا إِلَيْكَ الْكِتَابَ ۚ فَالَّذِينَ آتَيْنَاهُمُ الْكِتَابَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِ ۖ وَمِنْ هَٰؤُلَاءِ مَن يُؤْمِنُ بِهِ ۚ وَمَا يَجْحَدُ بِآيَاتِنَا إِلَّا الْكَافِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ka-dhālika anzalnā ilayka l-kitāba fa-lladhīna ātaynāhumu l-kitāba yuʾminūna bihi wa-min hāʾulāʾi man yuʾminu bihi wa-mā yajḥadu bi-āyātinā illā l-kāfirūna
versets.trad: Thus have We sent down to you the Book. Those to whom We gave the Scripture believe in it; and among these are some who believe in it. None reject Our signs save the disbelievers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sorts people into two camps before the Book: one whom We have looked upon with solicitude and shown mercy, and another, deprived, whom We have marked with the seal of wretchedness. Faith in the Revelation distinguishes the one whom grace has touched from the one who turns away.

mots.racine: ء ت ي / ك ت ب

versets.ar: وَمَا كُنتَ تَتْلُو مِن قَبْلِهِ مِن كِتَابٍ وَلَا تَخُطُّهُ بِيَمِينِكَ ۖ إِذًا لَّارْتَابَ الْمُبْطِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā kunta tatlū min qablihi min kitābin wa-lā takhuṭṭuhu bi-yamīnika idhan la-rtāba l-mubṭilūna
versets.trad: You did not recite any Book before this, nor write one with your right hand; otherwise the falsifiers would have doubted.
themes: the purity of the revealed heart
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the prophetic unletteredness the sign of a virgin heart: your heart was stripped of all acquired knowledge, your secret sanctified of every human mark; the Revelation reached you with no admixture of nature nor share of effort. When your heart and your secret were empty of every known thing and every trace, then descended upon you Our discourse and Our teaching, pure of all that is not from Us.

mots.racine: خ ط ط / ي م ن
mots.sens: nor write it with your right hand

versets.ar: بَلْ هُوَ آيَاتٌ بَيِّنَاتٌ فِي صُدُورِ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْعِلْمَ ۚ وَمَا يَجْحَدُ بِآيَاتِنَا إِلَّا الظَّالِمُونَ
versets.tr: bal huwa āyātun bayyinātun fī ṣudūri lladhīna ūtū l-ʿilma wa-mā yajḥadu bi-āyātinā illā ẓ-ẓālimūna
versets.trad: Rather it is clear signs in the breasts of those who have been given knowledge. None reject Our signs save the wrongdoers.
themes: hearts as treasuries of the secret
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the hearts of the elect among those who know God are the treasuries of the unseen: in them He has deposited the proofs of His right, the evidences of His secret, the indications of His Oneness, and the witnesses of His lordship. And he spins out the metaphor: the pearl is sought in the shell, for that is its abode; the sun in the constellations, for that is its rising; honey from the bee, for that is its hive — thus knowledge is sought in the hearts of His elect, for that is the canon of His knowledge.

mots.racine: ء ي ي / ب ي ن
ء ت ي / ع ل م

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا لَوْلَا أُنزِلَ عَلَيْهِ آيَاتٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِ ۖ قُلْ إِنَّمَا الْآيَاتُ عِندَ اللَّهِ وَإِنَّمَا أَنَا نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: wa-qālū lawlā unzila ʿalayhi āyātun min rabbihi qul innamā l-āyātu ʿinda llāhi wa-innamā anā nadhīrun mubīnun
versets.trad: They say: “If only signs had been sent down upon him from his Lord!” Say: “Signs are only with God, and I am only a clear warner.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī addresses: your state remained hidden from them, O Muḥammad, so they demanded visible proofs of you. But did the way We illumined for you, and the argument We gave you, recited to them without their being able to contest it or produce its like — did that not suffice them? To demand other miracles is the height of denial and ingratitude.

mots.sens: if only had been sent down

mots.racine: ن ذ ر / ب ي ن

versets.ar: أَوَلَمْ يَكْفِهِمْ أَنَّا أَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْكَ الْكِتَابَ يُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَرَحْمَةً وَذِكْرَىٰ لِقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: a-wa-lam yakfihim annā anzalnā ʿalayka l-kitāba yutlā ʿalayhim inna fī dhālika la-raḥmatan wa-dhikrā li-qawmin yuʾminūna
versets.trad: Does it not suffice them that We have sent down upon you the Book that is recited to them? Truly in that is a mercy and a reminder for a people who believe.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī continues: did it not suffice them that We made clear to you the way and furnished the proof recited to them, which they can neither contradict nor match? This is the true denial and the height of ingratitude. The Book itself is mercy and reminder for the believing heart.

mots.racine: ر ح م / ذ ك ر

versets.ar: قُلْ كَفَىٰ بِاللَّهِ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكُمْ شَهِيدًا ۖ يَعْلَمُ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۗ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِالْبَاطِلِ وَكَفَرُوا بِاللَّهِ أُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْخَاسِرُونَ
versets.tr: qul kafā bi-llāhi baynī wa-baynakum shahīdan yaʿlamu mā fī s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-lladhīna āmanū bi-l-bāṭili wa-kafarū bi-llāhi ulāʾika humu l-khāsirūna
versets.trad: Say: “God suffices as witness between me and you. He knows what is in the heavens and the earth. As for those who believe in falsehood and disbelieve in God — they are the losers.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gathers the firmness of the testimony: I am upon the truth, and God — glory to Him — knows it; you are not upon the truth, and God knows it. The all-knowing Witness decides between the Prophet and his opponents: to believe in the false and deny God is sheer loss.

mots.racine: ك ف ي / ش ه د
mots.def: God is the witness par excellence.

mots.racine: ء م ن / ب ط ل

versets.ar: وَيَسْتَعْجِلُونَكَ بِالْعَذَابِ ۚ وَلَوْلَا أَجَلٌ مُّسَمًّى لَّجَاءَهُمُ الْعَذَابُ وَلَيَأْتِيَنَّهُم بَغْتَةً وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-yastaʿjilūnaka bi-l-ʿadhābi wa-lawlā ajalun musamman la-jāʾahumu l-ʿadhābu wa-la-yaʾtiyannahum baghtatan wa-hum lā yashʿurūna
versets.trad: They press you to hasten the punishment. Were it not for an appointed term, the punishment would have come upon them; and it will surely come upon them suddenly, while they are unaware.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sums up the divine word: had I not assigned to every thing a term, I would have hastened it for them; but the punishment will come upon them — when it comes — abruptly and suddenly. The delay is no forgetting but wisdom: the sealed term eludes their awareness.

mots.racine: ء ج ل / س م و

versets.ar: يَسْتَعْجِلُونَكَ بِالْعَذَابِ وَإِنَّ جَهَنَّمَ لَمُحِيطَةٌ بِالْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: yastaʿjilūnaka bi-l-ʿadhābi wa-inna jahannama la-muḥīṭatun bi-l-kāfirīna
versets.trad: They press you to hasten the punishment; yet Hell will soon encompass the disbelievers.
versets.commentaire: They demand the hastening of the punishment, unaware that it already encircles them. Hell already encompasses the deniers: what they press for as distant already encloses them like a ring. Their very haste attests the blindness they are in, not seeing that the fire surrounds them on every side.

mots.racine: ح و ط / ك ف ر

versets.ar: يَوْمَ يَغْشَاهُمُ الْعَذَابُ مِن فَوْقِهِمْ وَمِن تَحْتِ أَرْجُلِهِمْ وَيَقُولُ ذُوقُوا مَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: yawma yaghshāhumu l-ʿadhābu min fawqihim wa-min taḥti arjulihim wa-yaqūlu dhūqū mā kuntum taʿmalūna
versets.trad: On the Day when the punishment will cover them from above them and from beneath their feet, and He will say: “Taste what you used to do!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the image in spiritual parallel: when the punishment encircles them — above the curse, beneath the swallowing, around them the disgrace — they find themselves clothed in the garment of abandonment, branded with the seal of deprivation, given to drink the draught of despair, crowned with the diadem of disappointment, fettered with the shackle of wrath. Such is the “Taste what you used to do.”

mots.racine: ت ح ت / ر ج ل

versets.ar: يَا عِبَادِيَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِنَّ أَرْضِي وَاسِعَةٌ فَإِيَّايَ فَاعْبُدُونِ
versets.tr: yā ʿibādiya lladhīna āmanū inna arḍī wāsiʿatun fa-iyyāya fa-ʿbudūni
versets.trad: O My servants who believe! Truly My earth is vast; so worship Me alone!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes the breadth of the earth an invitation to spiritual displacement: the world is too vast for a single place to stifle the seeker; if a dwelling turns hostile to him — for an acquired knowledge, a notoriety, a rank, an attachment, or some adverse trial — his way is to leave that place for another. Likewise the knower, he says, when the place no longer accords with his moment, transports himself to another.

mots.racine: ء ر ض / و س ع

versets.ar: كُلُّ نَفْسٍ ذَائِقَةُ الْمَوْتِ ۖ ثُمَّ إِلَيْنَا تُرْجَعُونَ
versets.tr: kullu nafsin dhāʾiqatu l-mawti thumma ilaynā turjaʿūna
versets.trad: Every soul shall taste death. Then to Us you will be returned.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes death a school of detachment: since it is so, repose is gained by lightening attachment to affairs; the believer's way is to accustom his soul to departure, ready and prepared. He cites: “If He said to me: die, I would say: hearing and obeying, and I would say to death's caller: welcome to you.” Not to hasten the term, not to find it heavy when it comes, but to live under the rule of the moment.

mots.racine: ذ و ق / م و ت

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ لَنُبَوِّئَنَّهُم مِّنَ الْجَنَّةِ غُرَفًا تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهَارُ خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا ۚ نِعْمَ أَجْرُ الْعَامِلِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti la-nubawwiʾannahum mina l-jannati ghurafan tajrī min taḥtihā l-anhāru khālidīna fīhā niʿma ajru l-ʿāmilīna
versets.trad: And those who believe and do righteous deeds — We shall surely lodge them in lofty chambers of the Garden, beneath which rivers flow, abiding therein forever. How excellent is the reward of the workers,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī transposes the chambers of the Garden into degrees of gnosis: they are today in the chambers of their knowings, upon the couches of their union, crowned with the diadems of their lordship, given to drink the cups of ecstasy, comforted in the gardens of nearness. The eternal abode is a promise, but also a presence already tasted by the hearts of the lovers.

mots.racine: ج ر ي / ن ه ر

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ صَبَرُوا وَعَلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ يَتَوَكَّلُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna ṣabarū wa-ʿalā rabbihim yatawakkalūna
versets.trad: those who are patient and who put their trust in their Lord.
versets.commentaire: Here Qushayrī sets his jewels on patience and reliance: patience is to stand with God while letting thought fall away; it is to hold the soul until its weaning; it is to swallow the cups of the decree without grimacing. And he climbs its degrees: first the painful effort, then the easy patience, then the endurance mingled with repose, finally the realization of contentment, where the servant, from bearer of the burden, becomes borne. As for tawakkul: joyful waiting, the repose of the secret in God, the turning of the heart away from all but the Lord.

versets.ar: وَكَأَيِّن مِّن دَابَّةٍ لَّا تَحْمِلُ رِزْقَهَا اللَّهُ يَرْزُقُهَا وَإِيَّاكُمْ ۚ وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
versets.tr: wa-ka-ayyin min dābbatin lā taḥmilu rizqahā llāhu yarzuquhā wa-iyyākum wa-huwa s-samīʿu l-ʿalīmu
versets.trad: How many a creature carries not its own provision — God provides for it and for you. And He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī glosses “carry not their provision”: they do not hoard it; whoever does not amass his provision in his purse or his storehouses, God provides for him without his suffering any toil. And he pushes further: God's will to keep you alive is stronger and more complete than your own concern to subsist; it does not befit your anxiety for your life to outweigh your Maker's provision for your survival.

mots.racine: ح م ل / ر ز ق

versets.ar: وَلَئِن سَأَلْتَهُم مَّنْ خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَسَخَّرَ الشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ لَيَقُولُنَّ اللَّهُ ۖ فَأَنَّىٰ يُؤْفَكُونَ
versets.tr: wa-la-in saʾaltahum man khalaqa s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍa wa-sakhkhara sh-shamsa wa-l-qamara la-yaqūlunna llāhu fa-annā yuʾfakūna
versets.trad: If you ask them, “Who created the heavens and the earth and subjected the sun and the moon?” they will surely say, “God.” How then are they deluded?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī brings their incoherence to light: asked about the Creator, they confess God; asked about the Provider, they no longer hold firm with God — here is a manifest contradiction! To acknowledge the creation and deny the providence is an unreason that belies itself.

mots.def: to be turned from the true.

versets.ar: اللَّهُ يَبْسُطُ الرِّزْقَ لِمَن يَشَاءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ وَيَقْدِرُ لَهُ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: llāhu yabsuṭu r-rizqa li-man yashāʾu min ʿibādihi wa-yaqdiru lahu inna llāha bi-kulli shayʾin ʿalīmun
versets.trad: God extends provision widely to whom He wills among His servants and straitens it for him. Truly God is Knowing of all things.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes two provisions: the provision of the outward — food and drink — and the provision of the innermost: the autonomy in spiritual meanings, such that the labor of speech cannot enclose it. And he observes that people are divided: some provided and at ease, some provided but straitened. The breadth or measure of the gift belongs to God's knowledge alone.

mots.racine: ب س ط / ر ز ق

versets.ar: وَلَئِن سَأَلْتَهُم مَّن نَّزَّلَ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ مَاءً فَأَحْيَا بِهِ الْأَرْضَ مِن بَعْدِ مَوْتِهَا لَيَقُولُنَّ اللَّهُ ۚ قُلِ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ۚ بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-la-in saʾaltahum man nazzala mina s-samāʾi māʾan fa-aḥyā bihi l-arḍa min baʿdi mawtihā la-yaqūlunna llāhu quli l-ḥamdu li-llāhi bal aktharuhum lā yaʿqilūna
versets.trad: If you ask them, “Who sends down water from the sky and revives the earth therewith after its death?” they will surely say, “God.” Say: “Praise be to God!” But most of them do not reason.
versets.commentaire: From the water that revives the earth Qushayrī draws a threefold resurrection: as they know that the life of the earth after its death comes from God through the rain, let them know that the life of souls after their death — at the gathering — comes from God's power; and likewise, the life of moments after their flight and the life of hearts after their languor come from the water of God's mercy.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / م و ه

mots.def: to give life to the dead soil.

versets.ar: وَمَا هَٰذِهِ الْحَيَاةُ الدُّنْيَا إِلَّا لَهْوٌ وَلَعِبٌ ۚ وَإِنَّ الدَّارَ الْآخِرَةَ لَهِيَ الْحَيَوَانُ ۚ لَوْ كَانُوا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā hādhihi l-ḥayātu d-dunyā illā lahwun wa-laʿibun wa-inna d-dāra l-ākhirata la-hiya l-ḥayawānu law kānū yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: This worldly life is nothing but diversion and play; the abode of the hereafter — that is the true Life, if only they knew.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: this world is like a dream; to leave it is to wake from sleep. The hereafter — there is life in its fullness, and the deliverance from desolation in its perfection and permanence. What men take for life is only a fleeting game; the true Life stands beyond the awakening.

mots.racine: ل ه و / ل ع ب
د و ر / ء خ ر

versets.ar: فَإِذَا رَكِبُوا فِي الْفُلْكِ دَعَوُا اللَّهَ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ الدِّينَ فَلَمَّا نَجَّاهُمْ إِلَى الْبَرِّ إِذَا هُمْ يُشْرِكُونَ
versets.tr: fa-idhā rakibū fī l-fulki daʿawu llāha mukhliṣīna lahu d-dīna fa-lammā najjāhum ilā l-barri idhā hum yushrikūna
versets.trad: When they board a ship, they call upon God, devoting their religion sincerely to Him; but when He delivers them to the land, behold, they associate others with Him.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī defines sincerity: ikhlāṣ is to empty the heart of all, the certainty that sincerity itself comes only from Him. In distress, when hope is cut off, they submit in supplication; but once the peril is lifted, they return to heedlessness and forget the state they were in. He cites: “When he recovers, he relapses into his ignorance, like the convalescent who falls back into his sickness.”

mots.racine: ر ك ب / ف ل ك
خ ل ص / د ي ن
ن ج و / ب ر ر

versets.ar: لِيَكْفُرُوا بِمَا آتَيْنَاهُمْ وَلِيَتَمَتَّعُوا ۖ فَسَوْفَ يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: li-yakfurū bimā ātaynāhum wa-li-yatamattaʿū fa-sawfa yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: Let them deny what We have given them and let them enjoy themselves; soon they will know.
versets.commentaire: The verse, a threatening dismissal, abandons them to their ingratitude: let them deny the favor received and enjoy for a time their passing pleasures — knowledge will come to them too late. The “soon they will know” is the seal suspended over the heedless one who has confused the gift with a due and grace with a possession.

mots.def: to be ungrateful for the favor.

versets.ar: أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا أَنَّا جَعَلْنَا حَرَمًا آمِنًا وَيُتَخَطَّفُ النَّاسُ مِنْ حَوْلِهِمْ ۚ أَفَبِالْبَاطِلِ يُؤْمِنُونَ وَبِنِعْمَةِ اللَّهِ يَكْفُرُونَ
versets.tr: a-wa-lam yaraw annā jaʿalnā ḥaraman āminan wa-yutakhaṭṭafu n-nāsu min ḥawlihim a-fa-bi-l-bāṭili yuʾminūna wa-bi-niʿmati llāhi yakfurūna
versets.trad: Do they not see that We have made a secure sanctuary, while people are snatched away all around them? Will they then believe in falsehood and deny the grace of God?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He favored them by warding off the trials from them and making the sanctuary secure; He reminded them of the immensity of His benefaction, then of their turning away from gratitude. The contrast is striking between the peace of the holy place and the tumult around it: to believe in the false while denying such grace is the height of blindness.

mots.racine: ح ر م / ء م ن
ن ع م / ك ف ر
mots.def: to be ungrateful for the favor.

versets.ar: وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ افْتَرَىٰ عَلَى اللَّهِ كَذِبًا أَوْ كَذَّبَ بِالْحَقِّ لَمَّا جَاءَهُ ۚ أَلَيْسَ فِي جَهَنَّمَ مَثْوًى لِّلْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-man aẓlamu mimmani ftarā ʿalā llāhi kadhiban aw kadhdhaba bi-l-ḥaqqi lammā jāʾahu a-laysa fī jahannama mathwan li-l-kāfirīna
versets.trad: Who is more unjust than he who fabricates a lie against God, or denies the Truth when it comes to him? Is there not in Hell an abode for the disbelievers?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: none is more unjust than he who forges a lie against God, turns away from the truth, prefers calumny, and does not attach himself to the realization of the true — those are the fallen in this world and the next. To lie about God and to deny the Truth when it has come is the extreme point of iniquity.

mots.racine: ف ر ي / ك ذ ب
ك ذ ب / ح ق ق
ث و ي / ك ف ر

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ جَاهَدُوا فِينَا لَنَهْدِيَنَّهُمْ سُبُلَنَا ۚ وَإِنَّ اللَّهَ لَمَعَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna jāhadū fīnā la-nahdiyannahum subulanā wa-inna llāha la-maʿa l-muḥsinīna
versets.trad: And those who strive for Us — We shall surely guide them to Our paths. And truly God is with the doers of good.
themes: God with the doers of good
versets.commentaire: The sealing verse of the sūra, where Qushayrī condenses the promise of guidance to the strivers: those who adorned their outward by striving had their inward beautified by contemplations; those who occupied their limbs with practices, We brought to their secrets the subtle graces; those who endured the toil for Us in the prayers, We requited with the rapture of union. And he grades the striving: first to forsake the forbidden, then the doubtful, then the superfluous, then to sever the attachments and purify oneself of distractions at every moment — to guard the senses for God and to count the breaths with God.

mots.def: the jihād in God and for God.

mots.racine: ه د ي / س ب ل
mots.sens: We shall guide them to Our paths

mots.def: al-muḥsinūn: those who excel in good.
